task:
<environment>
Browser is headless: yes
Uses existing browser cookies: no
Scope: Changes
Current branch: main
Main branch: main
</environment>

<developer_request>
Test markdown round-tripping and content integrity on http://localhost:5173?file=demo. This is a WYSIWYG Markdown editor with a source toggle (Cmd+/). 1) Load the demo content, then press Cmd+/ to switch to source view. Read the raw markdown. Switch back to WYSIWYG with Cmd+/ again — verify nothing was lost or corrupted. 2) In WYSIWYG mode, create a table by typing content, then switch to source view and verify the markdown table syntax is correct. 3) Create a task list with checkboxes (- [ ] items), check some boxes, switch to source view, verify [x] appears for checked items. 4) Type a code block with multiple languages (js, python, rust), verify syntax highlighting works for each. Switch to source and back — verify code content is preserved exactly. 5) Paste a complex markdown string with nested lists, blockquotes inside lists, and inline code inside bold — verify the WYSIWYG rendering handles nesting correctly. 6) Report every bug, content corruption, or rendering glitch you find.
</developer_request>

<scope_strategy>
- Treat committed and uncommitted work as one body of change.
- Cover the requested flow first, then the highest-risk adjacent flows.
- Test 2-4 follow-up flows, prioritizing paths that share components or data with the changed files.
- If the changes touch shared utilities or layouts, verify multiple pages that use them.
</scope_strategy>
